risk_model.P0: auth, tenant isolation, accounting truth, migrations, period close, idempotency/outbox

local.goal: Protect the normal edit/run loop with fast, deterministic, business-critical proof instead of a giant undifferentiated suite.
prove: The active slice still passes the curated dev-smoke/local-guard truthsuite manifests.
Fast-lane manifests remain fail-closed and still cover every stable critical non-flaky truthsuite test expected for local work.
No deployability claim is made from local-only evidence.
disallowed_proof: infra-heavy suites used as the default coding loop
broad CODE-RED or nightly-quality suites treated as mandatory for every edit

pr.goal: Keep routed PR CI as the authoritative merge-confidence lane without regressing the pre-existing bar.
prove: The routed PR CI path passes with changed-files traceability, catalog/quarantine guards, and critical truthsuite proof.
Broken-test classification and quarantine policy ran before merge confidence was claimed.
Fast local shortcuts did not weaken the remote PR merge baseline.
disallowed_proof: treating local-only success as merge approval
staging-only deployability evidence used to waive PR regressions

main.goal: Add slower but still stable integration proof after merge, rather than pushing all of it into every local or PR loop.
prove: The mainline gate-core lane passes critical, concurrency, and reconciliation truthsuite proof with hard guards.
Module-coverage and contract guards stay green for touched high-risk areas.
Integration confidence is stronger than PR confidence but still not a substitute for staging deployability evidence.
disallowed_proof: untagged flaky or low-signal suites promoted into blockers without classification

staging.goal: Prove the candidate artifact is deployable on the approved Flyway v2 path, not merely that tests happen to be green.
prove: The release-grade lane passes with release guards, migration checks, targeted high-risk proofs, and the deployable-state contract attached.
The candidate artifact demonstrates boot, health/readiness evidence, auth, tenant isolation, one O2C path, and one P2P/accounting path.
Contract guards and shared handoff/OpenAPI parity checks stay green alongside the release-grade lane.
Rollback owner, trigger, and rehearsal evidence remain explicit before promotion.
disallowed_proof: fast-lane success treated as deployability proof
degraded actuator health used to waive route-level runtime evidence
rollback omitted because the artifact compiled locally

canary.goal: Treat canary as production-like confirmation after staging passes, never as a substitute for staging proof.
prove: The already-staged artifact satisfies the deployable-state contract before receiving real traffic.
A narrow canary slice exercises auth, tenant isolation, one core O2C path, and one core P2P/accounting path under live telemetry.
Rollback-by-artifact-digest remains immediately available while the canary runs.
disallowed_proof: treating canary as a replacement for staging
nightly bulk-suite success claimed as live-traffic evidence
expanding traffic before rollback and ownership are explicit

deployable_state.summary: Deployable means the promoted artifact boots, migrates, protects tenant boundaries, proves one core O2C and one core P2P/accounting path, surfaces meaningful health/readiness evidence, and can roll back safely.

required_proof.description: Application boots on the promoted artifact/profile.
Flyway v2 migrations apply cleanly on the approved path.
Auth surfaces respond with the expected controlled contract.
One core order-to-cash path succeeds on the candidate artifact.
One core procure-to-pay/accounting path succeeds on the candidate artifact.
Health/readiness evidence is captured; degraded actuator health cannot waive route-level proof.
Rollback owner, trigger threshold, and rehearsal path are explicit before promotion.

broken_test_policy.summary: Broken tests are information, not ceremony. Every failing or flaky test touched by this mission must be classified before it can influence a confidence lane.

product-bug.meaning: The test exposed a real defect in production behavior or an invariant that the product currently violates.

bad-test.meaning: The assertion, fixture, or expectation is wrong or too stale to represent the real contract.

infra-coupled.meaning: The failure depends on an environment or dependency that makes the test too unstable for a fast lane.

allowed_actions.fix: Repair the product or the test and keep the strongest appropriate lane intact.
allowed_actions.quarantine: Allowed only with owner, repro_notes, start_date, expiry, classification, and action metadata in scripts/test_quarantine.txt; quarantined tests must stay out of gate-fast/core/release/reconciliation lane membership.
allowed_actions.delete: Remove the test only when it is proven obsolete and replaced by stronger proof or an explicit no-longer-needed contract.
allowed_actions.demote: Move the test to a slower or advisory lane when it is still useful but not stable enough for a fast lane.

broken_test_policy.fast_lane_rule: Fast lanes remain reserved for stable, business-critical proof. Slow, infra-heavy, or low-signal evidence belongs in stronger lanes or advisory observation loops instead of the default developer loop.
